concept: Record / Create or Preserve a Durable Account
description: To create or preserve a durable account of a stated event, decision, statement, observation, action, status, or source with relevant identity, time, provenance, version, and access conditions. Through the event-noun rule the word also names a record. Recording does not establish that the account is exact, complete, true, official, authentic, confidential, accessible, understood, delivered, or authoritative, and a later record does not erase conflicting testimony.
sound_symbolism: The complete marker-like opening 'sira' fixes an identifiable trace, while final 'mi' gathers the account so it can persist beyond present speakers. The inward close preserves continuity without claiming permanence, completeness, or truth.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'lo mia ha kanuro sirami.' (We record this decision.) State recorder, source, time, format, provenance, version, access, and amendment history where relevant. The event-noun rule supplies a record. Distinct from 'thekiro' (write), 'shane' (tell), 'somethi' (monitor), an exact source artifact outside Phi, 'shanewi' (notify), memory, and official status.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Records carry maintenance, ecological observation, allocation, commitments, and institutional memory beyond one meeting or office holder.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A bounded record term preserves provenance and competing testimony instead of allowing written form or official status to silence lived accounts.

tags.cognition: a durable account with source and version
tags.community: an institutional or shared record with access conditions
